title: Air-fryer cauliflower
subtitle: Spiced florets, charred edges, no mess.
excerpt: Cauliflower florets tossed in olive oil and smoked paprika, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C until charred at the edges and tender in the middle. Ready in 20 minutes, no turning needed. Serves two as a side.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer cooking method; the spice combination and 200°C/20-minute timing are based on standard commercial air-fryer roasting parameters for brassica vegetables.

content.text: Roasted cauliflower is one of the air fryer's best arguments. The concentrated dry heat chars the floret edges while the interior steams slightly from the moisture in the vegetable. It takes 18 minutes and smells like a proper roast.

The key is one layer in the basket and not overcrowding. A crowded basket traps steam and the florets go soft and pale instead of charred. For two portions, {{cauliflower}} of florets fits most standard 4 to 5 litre baskets in one layer. For four portions, cook in two batches; the second batch goes faster as the fryer is already hot.

items.name: Cauliflower
items.prepNote: cut into medium florets, about 3 to 4 cm

content.text: Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Put the florets from {{cauliflower}} cauliflower into a large bowl. Add {{olive-oil}} of olive oil, {{paprika-smoked}} of smoked paprika, and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Toss well until every piece is coated.

Tip the florets into the air-fryer basket in a single layer. Don't stack. Cook at 200°C for 16 to 18 minutes until the edges are dark and the stalks are tender when pierced with a knife. No shaking needed.
Tip the florets out onto a warm plate. The charred bits are the best parts. Serve straight away; the texture softens quickly once out of the fryer.

items.fix: One layer only. Preheat the basket; a cold start gives pale steamed results.
items.cause: Basket was too crowded, or fryer not preheated.

items.fix: Cut to 3 to 4 cm and keep to 200°C. Very large florets can be parboiled for 2 minutes first.
items.cause: Florets too large, or temperature too high.
items.symptom: Burnt on the outside, raw in the middle

items.fix: Pat the florets completely dry before tossing. The oil is the binder; don't skimp.
items.cause: Not enough oil, or florets were wet.

content.text: Swap smoked paprika for cumin and coriander for an Indian-adjacent note. A squeeze of lemon and a spoonful of tahini drizzled over after cooking gives a Middle Eastern direction. Parmesan grated over the hot florets in the last two minutes of cooking is very good.

Air-fryer cauliflower is a weeknight side that works next to grilled meat, in a grain bowl, in flatbreads, or eaten straight from the basket. The oven can match it in theory but rarely does in practice. The small basket holds the heat tight and gives a charring a big oven tray never quite does.
